Rationale: Syllabus, expectations, learning outcomes, how the course works (block structure, labs, capstone). Set the tone.
Why programming matters for engineers now. Landscape of AI tools. Where the industry is headed. This is the motivational hook.
Problem decomposition, abstraction, pseudocode. No Python, no IDE -- just structured thinking.
First engineering decomposition exercise. Groups of 3-4, guiding questions, present back. They do real intellectual work on Day 1/2.
Assumes installation is done. Tour the IDE, run a "hello world," use the terminal. Anyone who didn't install watches and catches up in office hours.
Review the week. Preview Week 2 (variables, types). Reflection form -- even on Week 1 this is valuable ("one thing that surprised you about this course").
